title: digitalis purpurea var. mariana
intro: Digitalis purpurea var. mariana, also known as Foxglove, is a biennial plant native to the British Isles, Western Europe, and North Africa, characterized by its tall spikes of purple flowers. The leaves and flowers of this plant contain digoxin and digitoxin, cardiac glycosides with medicinal properties that have been used to treat heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Foxglove include its ability to regulate heart rate, improve cardiac function, and reduce symptoms of heart failure, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. Foxglove can be prepared for medicinal use in various forms, including tinctures, teas, and infusions, although it's essential to use it under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
